action.reasoning: Observation: Best rmse_z500=465.47, all models plateau at val_loss~0.021. TTA (test-time augmentation with longitude shifts) is free and could help. Also, I should try training with a much larger model (base_ch=128, embed_dim=512) with stronger regularization. The key insight: I haven't tried saving multiple checkpoints and ensembling them. Let me: (1) Add TTA to predict.py to average over longitude shifts, (2) Train a larger model with stronger dropout and weight decay, (3) Save multiple checkpoints for ensemble. TTA is the easiest win - averaging over 4-8 longitude shifts reduces prediction variance.
action.command: MODEL_TYPE=hybrid BASE_CH=128 EMBED_DIM=512 DEPTH=10 DROPOUT=0.15 LR=5e-4 WEIGHT_DECAY=2e-4 NUM_EPOCHS=80 PATIENCE=25 RESIDUAL=1 USE_MIXUP=1 MIXUP_ALPHA=0.2 python train.py
